apps.name: Opinion Market
apps.description: A prediction market, but for opinions.
apps.longDescription: Opinion Market lets anyone open a market on a subjective question and trade shares on the outcomes they believe in. Stake on opinions, watch prices move as the crowd weighs in, and settle markets through community resolution. Every trade is an on-chain transaction on the Usernode chain, so the order book and your positions are fully transparent and verifiable.
apps.author: Usernode Labs
apps.logo: <svg viewBox='0 0 40 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect width='40' height='40' rx='10' fill='#2563eb'/><rect x='10' y='20' width='5' height='10' rx='1.5' fill='#fff'/><rect x='17.5' y='14' width='5' height='16' rx='1.5' fill='#fff'/><rect x='25' y='9' width='5' height='21' rx='1.5' fill='#fff'/></svg>

highlights.title: Open a market on anything
highlights.body: Pose any subjective question and let the crowd put a price on it.

highlights.title: Trade the odds
highlights.body: Buy and sell shares in outcomes as sentiment shifts in real time.

highlights.title: Community resolution
highlights.body: Markets settle through community consensus — no central referee.

highlights.title: Verifiable on-chain
highlights.body: Every trade and position is a transparent transaction on the Usernode chain.

apps.name: Falling Sands
apps.longDescription: Falling Sands is a real-time multiplayer falling-sand physics playground. Drop sand, water, fire, and other elements into a shared 2D world and watch them interact, spread, and collide with what everyone else is doing. State is synchronized across players so the canvas is always a collaborative mess of emergent chemistry.
apps.author: Usernode Labs

highlights.title: Pour the elements
highlights.body: Drop sand, water, and fire and watch real-time physics take over.

highlights.title: Play together
highlights.body: The canvas is shared — everyone's particles collide in one live world.

highlights.title: Emergent reactions
highlights.body: Elements interact, spread, and combust into surprising chain reactions.

highlights.title: A living sandbox
highlights.body: No goals, no limits — just a collaborative mess of emergent chemistry.

apps.name: Last One Wins
apps.description: Game where last one to add to the pot, gets the pot.
apps.longDescription: Last One Wins is a high-stakes game of nerve. Players add to a shared pot, and each contribution resets a countdown. When the timer finally runs out, whoever added last takes the entire pot. Every contribution is an on-chain transaction, so the pot and the leaderboard are provably fair and auditable on the Usernode chain.
apps.author: Usernode Labs

highlights.title: Add to the pot
highlights.body: Every contribution grows a shared jackpot anyone can win.

highlights.title: Reset the clock
highlights.body: Each new entry restarts the countdown — timing is everything.

highlights.title: Last one wins
highlights.body: When the timer hits zero, whoever went last takes the whole pot.

highlights.title: Provably fair
highlights.body: The pot and the leaderboard are auditable on the Usernode chain.

apps.name: Echo Diagnostic
apps.description: Simple test of sending / receiving transactions
apps.longDescription: Echo Diagnostic is a minimal utility for verifying end-to-end transaction flow on the Usernode chain. Send a transaction and watch it echo back, confirming that signing, broadcast, inclusion, and explorer indexing are all working. Handy for debugging wallet connectivity and as a reference implementation for the simplest possible dapp.
apps.author: Usernode Labs

highlights.title: Send a transaction
highlights.body: Fire off a test transaction straight from your linked wallet.

highlights.title: Watch it echo back
highlights.body: See the round-trip confirm signing, broadcast, and inclusion.

highlights.title: Confirm indexing
highlights.body: Verify the explorer picked up your transaction end to end.

highlights.title: A reference dapp
highlights.body: The simplest possible build — handy for debugging wallet connectivity.

apps.name: Mini Minecraft
apps.description: Place and break colored blocks in a tiny 2D voxel sandbox.
apps.longDescription: Mini Minecraft is a lightweight, browser-based block sandbox. Explore a freshly generated 2D world of grass, dirt, stone, and trees, then reshape it however you like: pick a block from the hotbar and click to place it, right-click (or grab the pickaxe) to break. Your world is saved in your browser, and you can roll a brand new one any time.
apps.author: Usernode Community
apps.logo: <svg viewBox='0 0 40 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect width='40' height='40' rx='10' fill='#5cab46'/><rect x='6' y='6' width='13' height='13' rx='2' fill='#8a5a32'/><rect x='21' y='6' width='13' height='13' rx='2' fill='#8d8d94'/><rect x='6' y='21' width='13' height='13' rx='2' fill='#e7d8a1'/><rect x='21' y='21' width='13' height='13' rx='2' fill='#3e7d3a'/></svg>

highlights.title: Place blocks
highlights.body: Pick grass, stone, wood, water, and more from the hotbar and click to build.

highlights.title: Break anything
highlights.body: Right-click or grab the pickaxe to mine through dirt, stone, and trees.

highlights.title: Fresh worlds
highlights.body: Every new world rolls its own hills, trees, and buried coal.

highlights.title: Saved in your browser
highlights.body: Your creation persists between visits — or reset for a clean slate.

apps.name: Quick
apps.description: Catch falling blocks in this retro handheld console game with 90s pixel-art style.
apps.longDescription: Quick is a nostalgic handheld gaming experience inspired by classic Game Boy and Digimon-era handhelds. Move your catcher left and right to catch falling colored blocks before they hit the ground. The game accelerates as you score, testing your reflexes in an authentically retro pixel-art environment with warm plastic console aesthetics.
apps.author: Usernode Community
apps.logo: <svg viewBox='0 0 40 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect width='40' height='40' rx='8' fill='#2d3142'/><rect x='4' y='4' width='32' height='20' rx='2' fill='#0d3b39'/><circle cx='10' cy='32' r='2' fill='#a8b3c7'/><circle cx='14' cy='32' r='2' fill='#a8b3c7'/><circle cx='30' cy='28' r='2.5' fill='#d4634a'/><circle cx='30' cy='34' r='2.5' fill='#e8d944'/><circle cx='36' cy='28' r='2.5' fill='#5c96d9'/><circle cx='36' cy='34' r='2.5' fill='#48a867'/></svg>

highlights.title: Classic handheld gameplay
highlights.body: Move left and right to catch falling blocks — simple but addictive.

highlights.title: Retro console aesthetics
highlights.body: Play on an authentic 90s-inspired handheld device with plastic bezel and buttons.

highlights.title: Pixel-art visuals
highlights.body: Enjoy crisp, low-resolution graphics that capture the charm of classic handhelds.

highlights.title: Quick to learn, hard to master
highlights.body: Progressive difficulty keeps the challenge fresh as your score climbs.
